channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Indiana man to learn his sentence following conviction in 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike will face up to 130 years in prison when a judge sentences him Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turkey and Russia engage in delicate maneuvers over Syria after Assad’s downfall
item.description: The rapid downfall of Syrian leader Bashar Assad has touched off a new round of delicate geopolitical maneuvering between Russia's Vladimir Putin and Turkey's Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Closures, Social Security checks, furloughs: What a government shutdown might mean
item.description: Congress has until midnight Friday to come up with a way to fund the government or federal agencies will shut down, meaning hundreds of thousands of federal employees could be sent home — or stay on the job without pay — just ahead of the holidays.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Docs: Employee who stabbed company president wore mask, led police on chase
item.description: Court documents are revealing new details about the stabbing of a company president by one of his own employees near Muskegon.
item.dc:creator: Byron Tollefson, NewsNation

item.title: FDA updates definition of 'healthy' on food labels for first time in 30 years
item.description: A new rule from the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) will update what it means for food to be labeled “healthy” for the first time in 30 years, a move that aligns with current nutrition science and is meant to help consumers better navigate food labels.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: Chinese national charged with acting as Beijing's agent in local California election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Chinese national was arrested Thursday on charges of acting as an illegal agent for Beijing when serving as the campaign manager for an unnamed politician elected to a city council in Southern California two years ago.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, AMY TAXIN and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Snow, ice and strong winds make for dangerous conditions in upper Midwest
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A fast-moving winter storm brought snow, ice, strong winds and bitter cold to much of the upper Midwest, snarling traffic in the Twin Cities and forcing North Dakota officials to shut down an interstate highway.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor pleads not guilty in bribery case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The New York City mayor's former chief adviser, her son and two real estate investors were indicted Thursday on bribery and conspiracy charges that came as the mayor himself fights a separate corruption case.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 killed, 3 injured when freight train derails in small West Texas city after collision
item.description: Two Union Pacific employees were killed and three people were injured when a freight train collided with a tractor-trailer and derailed in a small West Texas city, authorities said Thursday. The train derailed around 5 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE and JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump border czar: 100K beds, more ICE agents needed for deportations
item.description: “This will be an expense — this will be an expensive operation. But in the long run, it should be a — it would be a huge tax savings on the American people,” Homan said.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers, business owners, fire survivors face uncertainty after $100B in disaster relief flounders
item.description: American farmers, small business owners and wildfire survivors are among those who will suffer if Congress cannot agree on a new spending bill after President-elect Donald Trump abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan that included more than $100 billion in disaster aid.
item.dc:creator: Travis Loller And Leah Willingham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House GOP eyes plan B stopgap with separate 2-part debt limit commitment
item.description: House Republicans are eyeing an alternative government funding stopgap that excludes a debt ceiling increase, and are instead working on a commitment to raise the borrowing limit twice next year in a bid to appease President-elect Trump, two sources told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon workers strike at facilities around the country as Teamsters seek contract
item.description: Amazon says it doesn’t expect an impact on its operations during what the union calls the largest strike against the company in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles deputy mayor allegedly made bomb threat to City Hall
item.description: Federal agents searched the home of Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Brian Williams on Tuesday following allegations that he made a bomb threat to City Hall earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Texas train derails after hitting tractor-trailer, barreling into city building: video
item.description: One person is dead and four others are injured after a train hit a tractor-trailer on the railroad tracks in Pecos, Texas, causing it to derail and strike the city's Chamber of Commerce building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee dog to be reunited with family after agencies team up to rescue him from ravine: video
item.description: A Tennessee dog by the name of Apollo will be back home for the holidays after he was found by local and state agencies injured and stuck in a ravine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man accused in UnitedHealthcare CEO killing faces federal charge that's eligible for death penalty
item.description: The man arrested in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO is being held without bail after a New York federal court appearance on charges including a death-eligible murder count.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo And Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 pilots die in fiery small plane crash into vacant building near Honolulu airport
item.description: Shortly after taking off from Honolulu's Daniel K. Inouye International Airport, two pilots were killed when their small plane on a training flight crashed into a vacant building.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Grubhub to pay $25M to settle claims of tricking customers and drivers
item.description: Federal Trade Commission officials said Grubhub engaged in illegal practices by misleading customers about the cost of delivery and the benefits of a Grubhub subscription.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Walmart testing body cameras for some in-store employees
item.description: Some in-store Walmart employees are wearing body cameras as part of a pilot program, according to a company spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, KTLA 5

item.title: Things to know about the shooting at a Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: Police in Madison, Wisconsin, are trying to determine what prompted a 15-year-old student to open fire at a private Christian school. The shooting Monday was just days before the winter break. A fellow student and a teacher were killed. The shooter also died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster for 'election interference' after pre-election poll
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump sued the Des Moines Register and its pollster for “brazen election interference” in publishing a survey the weekend before the election that showed Democrat Kamala Harris with a surprising lead of three percentage points in the state. The Register's parent Gannett Co.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How to claim a 2nd payment from the Equifax data breach settlement
item.description: Equifax has started alerting people of more money waiting for them from a massive fund the credit reporting agency paid to settle a data breach.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Family of slain Cash App founder Bob Lee reacts to Nima Momeni verdict
item.description: On Tuesday morning, a San Francisco jury announced it had found Nima Momeni guilty of second degree murder in the death of Cash App founder Bob Lee.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, Fox 5

item.title: Judge allows testing of tissue from George Floyd as officer convicted of killing him seeks new trial
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A judge has granted permission to lawyers for Derek Chauvin to have samples from George Floyd examined as part of the former Minneapolis police officer's efforts to challenge his conviction on a federal civil rights charge stemming from Floyd's death in 2020. U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, WGN 9

item.title: Bud Light no longer the best-selling draft beer at bars, data suggests
item.description: The overtaking of Bud Light as America's most popular draft beer has been "years in the making," according to the founder of Draftline Technologies, a company that provides software for beverage distributors.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Biden establishes a national monument for Frances Perkins, the 1st female Cabinet secretary
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Monday established a national monument honoring the late FDR-era Labor Secretary Frances Perkins, the first woman to serve in a presidential Cabinet and a driving force behind Franklin D. Roosevelt's New Deal programs after the Great Depression.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, AAMER MADHANI and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mysterious drones leave officials perplexed
item.description: With drone sightings spreading through the East Coast, federal officials are helping local and state authorities.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, WGN 9

item.title: Millionaire boomers less likely to share their wealth: Charles Schwab
item.description: Data revealed that baby boomers, born from 1946 to 1964, plan to spend most of their wealth rather than reserve it for future generations.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, WGN 9

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9
